reasoning: The threshold requires 'Violence against civilians' to exceed 10 times the reference numerical value in a single 30-day period. With the reference value at 10.500, the 30-day average over the preceding 360 days is exactly 9.5, equating to about 114 events over the past year. Thus, the condition requires >105 events to happen in a single month—nearly matching the entire annual total. Even during intense historical flashpoints (such as the recent July 2024 post-election crackdown), event counts rarely skew so astronomically in a single 30-day window, especially with documented NGO suppression currently crippling data collection. Given the lack of a major new >10x outbreak of civilian massacres prior to the cutoff, achieving this for any of the upcoming dates is highly improbable. Probabilities are strictly bounded near the minimum permissible, escalating slightly for more distant dates.
